subject: Change Policy PAP & API to https by default
commit_message:
Change Policy PAP & API to https by default

1) Changing policy PAP & API components to start in https mode by
default.
2) Changing supportedPolicyTypes for apex to fix the ineterference between
apex & drools for legacy operational policies.
3) Changing the replicaCount of xacml to 3 so that we have enough
instances of it running to support controlloop, monitoring &
optimization PdpGroups.

Change-Id: I5271878db4c4adebc418ef63ecb09880c02082ff
Issue-ID: POLICY-1693
Signed-off-by: ramverma <ram.krishna.verma@est.tech>

Patch Set 2:

> ./oom/kubernetes/robot/resources/config/eteshare/config/integration_robot_properties.py
 > ./oom/kubernetes/robot/resources/config/eteshare/config/vm_properties.py
 > and
 > ./oom/kubernetes/robot/values.yaml
 > 
 > should be updated at the same time

I think these changes can be made independently.  That robot script does not currently interact with the new Policy PAP, thus changing the new PAP to use https should have no bearing on it.  The robot script can be changed, in another review, to point to the new PAP.

Patch Set 2:

Let me try to clarify and summarize feedback from team, Brian, hopefully will make sense.  Let us know otherwise.

There are several pieces of work at play that would happen in this and several other future reviews:

1.  This work to meet the security requirements that all interfaces are using https.   This affects to the new api, pap, xacml-pdp and apex-pdp that were uncovered to be using http in oom installations.   This work is just to satisfy those security requirements.

2. As CLAMP and DCAE right now uses http to talk to new PAP and API, and xacml-pdp policy components, they will have to change to use https instead.   So, there are hopefully small changes on their side.   This is a side effect of this review per (1).

3. Robot is not impacted by this review, as the current interfaces that it interfaces with don't have changes.   Robot interfaces with drools and legacy pdp-x, and those interfaces won't change.

4. At some point, pending work on our side, will have a suitable replacement for new interfaces with new components to do healthcheck, vfirewall, etc ..   That's not in place right now as it is being worked out.   At this point, there's no changes, but once that work is completed, and integrated, it will require updating of the robot interface simultaneously.  But this is future work, and there's no side effects from this review into robot as of now.
